pattern: Compute the total tree sum in a first DFS pass, then a second pass finds the subtree sum s that maximises s*(total-s), returning that maximum product modulo 10^9+7.
hints: After cutting one edge you get two subtrees whose sums must add up to the total tree sum. If one subtree has sum s the other has sum (total - s).
The product s*(total-s) is maximised when s is as close to total/2 as possible. Collect all subtree sums in a first traversal, then scan them for the best split.
Because the answer can be huge, return the maximum product before taking the modulo, then return that value mod 10^9+7.

edge_inputs.description: all nodes have value 1 — many equal products
left-heavy tree — best cut is the right child edge
two nodes with max value — product is large

stress.description: random complete-ish binary tree near the node cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 200))
    vals = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    # Build BFS-order (level-order) list — always a complete binary tree shape
    return [vals]
